title: Add manifest type conversion to kpod push
body:
User can select from 3 manifest types: oci, v2s1, or v2s2
e.g kpod push --format v2s2 alpine dir:my-directory
Added "compress" flag to enable compression when true

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: So, if I'm understanding this correctly this is adding support for manifest type in kpod push? Why? isn't kpod push always supposed to push to a docker registry? (with v2s2), if that's the case and there's no real use case to add oci support to kpod push (cause there's no oci enabled registry at the moment), I would actually avoid this PR :)

@runcom kpod push supports the following transports: docker-archive, oci-archive, dir, and docker and atomic registries. So this PR is adding the manifest type conversion feature for pushing images to a dir, same as skopeo does.
We added this feature to buildah push as well.

@rhatdan @runcom PTAL.
Added a check so that the --compress and --format flags can only be used with the "dir" transport.
Elaborated a bit more on the transports in the doc.
Please let me know if anything more is needed to make this as clear as possible.

Does the forcecompress work.  You state that if I don't set the compress flag then it will stay in the state of the source.  
If I have a source:
1 Source Uncompressed and state forcecompress - Dest should be compressed.
2 Source Uncompressed and dont state forcecompress - Dest should be uncmpressed
3 Source Compressed and I want it uncompressed how do I do this?
But what would be the syntax for the user.  If he sets the compress=false flag, he would expect the dest to be uncompressed, except there is nothing that supports this in containers/image?
